instruction: Always call verify_build(agentId) immediately after completing a fleet merge — before reporting success to Daniel. Syntax errors on master block the station.
Rate every build agent you complete in the same session using rate_build(agentId, rating, notes). Do not defer to a future session. Ratings accumulate the quality signal.
At startup or when Daniel says 'begin' without specifying a task, call load_proposals() first. HIGH proposals should become active builds before new proposals are created.
When you notice an architecture decision, a non-obvious fix, or a pattern worth remembering, call capture_insight immediately. Do not wait for auto-crystallization at turn 5.
All multi-session work belongs to a named project (project_create/project_status). Check project_status() at session start so ongoing work is never abandoned silently.

When Daniel corrects your direction, rejects an approach, or confirms a non-obvious choice — call capture_insight(insight, category:"feedback") immediately before replying. Include: what he corrected, what the right direction is, and why it matters.

At the end of a productive session (when you are summarizing to Daniel), call capture_insight with a 2-3 sentence session narrative: what changed this session, what direction Daniel gave, what to carry into the next session. This is the thread that survives context resets.

text: Quarterly autodidact cycle: call build_outcomes() first to identify failure patterns, then implement 2-3 targeted improvements addressing the most common failures. Always run run_tests() after merging. Ground every proposal in observed data not speculation.
